description_long: This is a traditional Turkish or Middle Eastern-style coffee kettle, known as a cezve or ibrik, crafted primarily from hammered copper with a rich, reddish-brown patina. The body is bulbous and tapers toward the top, with a wide, open mouth and a slightly flared rim. A long, turned wooden handle is attached to the side, featuring a metal ring at the end for hanging or handling, and is affixed with a metal collar. The surface of the kettle is textured with visible hammer marks, giving it a rustic, handcrafted appearance. There is a small, protruding spout for pouring, integrated into the upper body. No lid is visible, and the base is rounded and slightly flattened for stability.
description: A hammered copper cezve coffee kettle with a bulbous body, long wooden handle, and small pouring spout. The surface is textured and rustic, with no visible lid.
description_view_0: Angled view showing the bulbous copper body, flared rim, and long wooden handle extending to the right.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the end of the wooden handle with a metal ring and the rounded back of the kettle.
description_view_2: Side view displaying the full length of the handle and the kettle's textured copper surface.
description_view_3: Front view focusing on the open mouth, flared rim, and the handle extending directly away from the viewer.
